data.title: Named agent dispatch fails for dso:intent-search — Agent tool rejects plugin agent types
data.description:
## Bug Description

Named plugin agents (e.g., dso:intent-search) fail when dispatched via the Agent tool. The Agent tool only recognizes built-in agent types (general-purpose, statusline-setup, Explore, Plan, claude-code-guide) and rejects plugin-defined agents with:

Error: Agent type 'dso:intent-search' not found. Available agents: general-purpose, statusline-setup, Explore, Plan, claude-code-guide

## Reproduction

1. Invoke /dso:fix-bug on any bug ticket
2. Gate 1a dispatches dso:intent-search via Agent tool with subagent_type: dso:intent-search
3. Agent tool rejects the subagent_type

## Impact

This is a recurring failure observed in a host project. The fallback (reading the agent .md file inline) works but loses the Agent tool's isolation and model selection benefits. All named plugin agents in the agent routing table are affected, not just dso:intent-search.

## Investigation Notes

This is an escalation — a previous attempt to fix this issue was unsuccessful. The root cause appears to be that the Claude Code Agent tool does not have a mechanism to register custom/plugin agent types. The agent-routing.conf and plugins/dso/agents/*.md files define agents, but the Agent tool's subagent_type parameter only accepts its hardcoded built-in list.

## Expected Behavior

Agent tool should either (a) accept plugin-defined agent types from the agents/ directory, or (b) the plugin framework should provide a registration mechanism for custom agent types.

## Actual Behavior

Agent tool rejects all non-built-in subagent_type values. Plugin agents fall back to inline .md file reading.
